title: Refactoring
description: Systematically improve the codebase without changing observable behaviour. Identifies duplication, architectural drift, performance bottlenecks, and accessibility gaps — then produces an atomic commit plan so every improvement is reviewable and reversible.
prerequisite: 07-code-scaffolding complete. Run after any significant feature addition or every 2-3 days of active development.
instructions: Before touching any code, read the full file tree and produce a written audit report. Never refactor silently.
Categorise every finding by type: Duplication, Architecture Drift, Performance, Accessibility, Type Safety, Dead Code, Security.
Assign each finding a priority: P1 (blocks correctness), P2 (degrades maintainability), P3 (nice to have).
Present the audit report to the user. Get confirmation on which findings to action before writing any code.
Implement each fix as a single atomic commit with a conventional commit message. Never bundle unrelated fixes.
After each commit, run tsc --noEmit and the test suite. A refactor that breaks tests is not a refactor — it is a bug.
Update any affected JSDoc comments and inline TODOs after each fix.
Do not change component APIs or config file shapes during refactoring — those are breaking changes requiring a new ADR.

duplication: Are there repeated fetch patterns across route files that should be extracted to lib/content.ts?
Are className strings duplicated across components that should become shared CSS modules?
Are Zod schemas duplicated that should be composed via z.extend() or z.merge()?
Are metadata generation patterns duplicated across pages that should use lib/seo.ts helpers?
architecture_drift: Are any client components importing from lib/content.ts or lib/integrations.ts (server-only)?
Are any config values being read outside of their designated config/ files?
Are domain boundaries being violated — e.g. blog components importing portfolio types?
Are feature flag checks scattered across files instead of going through lib/features.ts?
performance: Are images using next/image with explicit width and height?
Are large client components that could be React Server Components?
Are fonts loaded via next/font/google (zero layout shift) rather than @import?
Are generateStaticParams implemented for all dynamic routes?
Are revalidate values set appropriately for ISR routes?
accessibility: Do all interactive elements have accessible labels (aria-label or visible text)?
Is the heading hierarchy correct on every page (single h1, logical h2/h3 nesting)?
Do all images have meaningful alt text (or alt='' for decorative images)?
Is focus management correct for the mobile nav open/close?
Does the theme toggle announce its state change to screen readers?
type_safety: Are there any `any` types that can be replaced with specific types or generics?
Are there non-null assertions (!) that should be replaced with proper null checks?
Are environment variables typed via a validated env schema (e.g. t3-env or manual Zod)?
Are MDX frontmatter fields validated at build time via the Zod schemas from lib/validators.ts?
dead_code: Are there unused imports (caught by ESLint no-unused-vars)?
Are there commented-out code blocks that should be deleted?
Are there TODO comments from phase 7 that have been implemented but not removed?
Are there unused CSS custom properties in tokens.css?
security: Are all external URLs validated before rendering as href or src?
Are API route inputs still validated with Zod (re-check after any route modification)?
Are Content Security Policy headers still correctly configured after adding any new third-party scripts?
Has npm audit been run — are there any new high/critical vulnerabilities?

examples: perf(images): add explicit dimensions to all WorkCard cover images
refactor(types): replace any with WorkItem in WorkDetail props
fix(a11y): add aria-label to ThemeToggle button
refactor(security): centralise external URL validation in lib/validators.ts

outputs: Written audit report presented to user before any code changes
One atomic commit per finding actioned
Updated JSDoc comments on any modified functions
Clean tsc --noEmit and passing test suite after all commits
guardrails: Never change observable behaviour during a refactor. If behaviour must change, that is a feature — open a new issue.
Never bundle more than one logical change in a single commit.
Never refactor a file you do not have a test for — write the test first (see skill 09).
Never remove a TODO without either implementing it or opening a GitHub issue to track it.
If a refactor touches a config file shape, stop — that requires a new ADR and a migration guide.
